docs: One entry per state SLA Capital lends in. `slug` becomes the URL segment. `metros` are the 7 most active submarkets we mention; the 8th tile is auto-added as 'Everywhere else — statewide coverage'. `signals` are the three market-truths we lead the 'Why <State>' section with. `taxNote` mentions no state income tax where applicable — omit for states with income tax. `whyInvestor` is the answer to the 'is <State> a good state for investors?' FAQ — write it state-specific and confident.

states.name: Alabama

metros.name: Huntsville
metros.detail: Madison County — Redstone Arsenal + Cummings Research Park

metros.name: Birmingham
metros.detail: Jefferson County — Hoover, Vestavia Hills, Trussville, Homewood

metros.name: Mobile
metros.detail: Mobile County — Gulf Coast SFR + small-multi

metros.name: Montgomery
metros.detail: Montgomery County — state capital + Maxwell AFB demand

metros.name: Tuscaloosa
metros.detail: Tuscaloosa County — University of Alabama market

metros.name: Auburn / Opelika
metros.detail: Lee County — Auburn University + Kia Georgia adjacencies

signals.title: Huntsville tech + defense growth
signals.body: Huntsville has been among the fastest-growing US metros for years, driven by aerospace, defense, and technology employment around Redstone Arsenal, NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, and Cummings Research Park.

signals.title: Affordable acquisition basis
signals.body: Birmingham, Montgomery, and Alabama secondary markets offer entry price-points that make the rent-to-price math work. Real cash-on-cash returns even after debt service.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly law
signals.body: Alabama uses non-judicial foreclosure and has landlord-friendly statutes with predictable timelines. Holding costs stay projectable for cash-flow modeling.

states.whyInvestor: Alabama is a top cash-flow state in the Southeast. Huntsville tech and defense growth anchor sustained rental demand. Birmingham offers deep SFR inventory at affordable basis. Non-judicial foreclosure and landlord-friendly law keep holds projectable.

states.name: Arkansas

metros.name: Little Rock
metros.detail: Pulaski County — state capital + healthcare hub

metros.name: Northwest Arkansas
metros.detail: Bentonville, Fayetteville, Rogers, Springdale — Walmart HQ region

metros.name: Conway
metros.detail: Faulkner County — Little Rock-adjacent + UCA demand

signals.title: Northwest Arkansas Walmart economy
signals.body: Bentonville and the surrounding Northwest Arkansas metro have grown around Walmart's global HQ, its supplier network, and Tyson Foods. Sustained employment growth drives real rental demand.

signals.title: Affordable acquisition basis
signals.body: Little Rock, Fort Smith, and Arkansas secondary markets offer entry price-points that make cash-flow math work. Real cash-on-cash returns for buy-and-hold investors.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly
signals.body: Arkansas statutes are considered landlord-friendly with predictable eviction timelines. Holding costs stay projectable.

states.whyInvestor: Arkansas is a strong cash-flow market. Northwest Arkansas (Walmart HQ + supplier ecosystem) anchors long-term employment and rental demand. Little Rock adds state-capital + healthcare stability. Affordable acquisition basis makes the yield math work.

states.name: Colorado

metros.name: Denver Metro
metros.detail: Denver County + Aurora, Lakewood, Thornton, Westminster

metros.name: Colorado Springs
metros.detail: El Paso County — Peterson SFB + Fort Carson demand

metros.name: Fort Collins
metros.detail: Larimer County — Colorado State University market

signals.title: Sustained tech + defense employment
signals.body: Denver and Colorado Springs anchor deep tech, aerospace, and defense employer bases (Lockheed Martin, Ball Aerospace, USSF, Northrop Grumman) that support rental demand across the Front Range.

signals.title: Population inflow
signals.body: Colorado continues to attract in-migration from higher-cost coastal metros. Rental demand along the Front Range stays deep across every metro.

signals.title: Mountain STR corridor
signals.body: Colorado ski and mountain markets host active short-term rental corridors where local zoning permits. Our DSCR program supports STR income scenarios.

states.whyInvestor: Colorado is a sustained-growth investor state. Front Range tech + defense employment (Denver, Colorado Springs, Boulder) anchors long-term rental demand. Population inflow keeps market depth strong. Mountain STR corridors add flexibility for investors who want STR + LTR mixed strategies.

states.name: Connecticut

metros.name: Waterbury
metros.detail: New Haven County — SFR + small-multi cash-flow

metros.name: Danbury
metros.detail: Fairfield County — Western CT commuter market

metros.name: Stamford
metros.detail: Fairfield County — NYC financial-services commuter demand

signals.title: NYC-metro commuter demand
signals.body: Fairfield County (Stamford, Norwalk, Bridgeport, Danbury) is a core NYC commuter belt. Rental demand stays deep among finance, tech, and healthcare professionals working in Manhattan.

signals.title: Insurance + healthcare anchors
signals.body: Hartford is one of the largest insurance-industry hubs in the US. Stable employment drives predictable long-term rental demand across the metro.

signals.title: Yale + healthcare in New Haven
signals.body: Yale University, Yale-New Haven Hospital, and a growing biotech corridor anchor rental demand across the New Haven metro.

states.whyInvestor: Connecticut is a mixed-strategy investor market. Fairfield County commuter demand drives premium rentals for NYC-metro workers. Hartford insurance and New Haven Yale/biotech provide stable employment anchors. Rental demand stays consistent across every major metro.

states.name: Delaware

metros.name: Wilmington
metros.detail: New Castle County — banking + Philly-metro adjacencies

metros.name: Dover
metros.detail: Kent County — state capital + Dover AFB demand

metros.name: Newark
metros.detail: New Castle County — University of Delaware market

metros.name: Rehoboth Beach
metros.detail: Sussex County — coastal LTR + STR corridor

metros.name: Bear & Smyrna
metros.detail: SFR corridor between Wilmington and Dover

signals.title: Banking + business anchor
signals.body: Wilmington hosts a deep banking, financial services, and corporate legal ecosystem (Delaware's business-friendly corporate law drives concentration of Chancery Court cases and Fortune 500 registrations).

signals.title: Coastal STR opportunity
signals.body: Rehoboth Beach, Lewes, Bethany Beach, and the Sussex County coastal corridor host active short-term rental markets. Our DSCR program supports STR income where zoning permits.

signals.title: Philadelphia adjacencies
signals.body: Northern Delaware is effectively a Philadelphia commuter belt. Rental demand from Philly-metro workers stays consistent across Wilmington and its suburbs.

states.whyInvestor: Delaware is a compact but dense investor market. Wilmington banking + Philly-metro commuter demand drives premium rentals. Rehoboth Beach and the Sussex County coast add STR opportunity. Consistent rental demand across the state.

states.name: Florida

metros.name: Miami–Dade
metros.detail: Miami, Miami Beach, Doral, Hialeah — LTR + STR scenarios

metros.name: Orlando
metros.detail: Metro Orlando — including Kissimmee, Davenport, Lake Nona (STR corridor)

metros.name: Jacksonville
metros.detail: Duval County, Ponte Vedra, St. Augustine

metros.name: Fort Lauderdale
metros.detail: Broward County, Coral Springs, Hollywood, Pompano Beach

metros.name: Cape Coral–Fort Myers
metros.detail: Southwest Florida — Naples, Bonita Springs, Estero

signals.title: No state income tax
signals.body: Rental income and capital gains from Florida properties are not subject to state income tax — a meaningful yield boost over the hold period.

signals.title: Sustained population inflow
signals.body: Florida continues to attract high volumes of domestic migration — retirees, remote workers, and families relocating from higher-tax states. Rental demand stays deep across every metro.

signals.title: STR + LTR flexibility
signals.body: Metros like Orlando, Miami, and the Gulf Coast have active short-term rental markets alongside long-term rentals — Florida investors often run mixed strategies on the same portfolio.

states.whyInvestor: Florida is one of the largest single-family rental markets in the U.S. No state income tax, sustained population inflow, and strong demand for both long-term and short-term rentals. Florida landlord-tenant law is generally landlord-friendly at the state level.

states.name: Georgia

metros.name: Metro Atlanta
metros.detail: Marietta, Alpharetta, Kennesaw, Sandy Springs, Roswell, Duluth

metros.name: Atlanta South & East
metros.detail: Snellville, Lawrenceville, Lithonia, Stone Mountain, Douglasville

metros.name: Savannah
metros.detail: Chatham County — LTR + coastal STR scenarios

metros.name: Augusta
metros.detail: Richmond & Columbia counties — Fort Eisenhower demand

signals.title: Affordable acquisition basis
signals.body: Metro Atlanta and Georgia secondary markets still offer entry-price points well below coastal metros — enabling meaningful cash-on-cash returns for buy-and-hold investors and workable margins on BRRRR strategies.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly law
signals.body: Georgia is a non-judicial foreclosure state with a predictable eviction timeline and landlord-friendly statutes. Holding costs stay projectable, which matters for cash-flow modeling on rental portfolios.

signals.title: Sustained metro growth
signals.body: Metro Atlanta continues to lead the Southeast in job creation and population inflow, supporting deep long-term rental demand across every submarket in the region.

states.whyInvestor: Metro Atlanta is one of the most active BRRRR markets in the country — a combination of affordable acquisition basis, deep rental demand from sustained population growth, and a non-judicial foreclosure process that keeps holding costs predictable. Georgia's landlord-tenant statutes are considered landlord-friendly.

states.name: Hawaii

metros.name: Honolulu (Oahu)
metros.detail: Waikiki, Kapolei, Kailua — LTR + STR where zoning permits

metros.name: West Oahu
metros.detail: Ewa Beach, Waipahu, Kapolei — SFR corridor

metros.name: Maui
metros.detail: Kihei, Lahaina, Wailea — STR + LTR mix

metros.name: Kauai
metros.detail: Lihue, Poipu, Princeville — resort STR corridor

signals.title: STR corridor
signals.body: Hawaii's resort corridors host some of the most active short-term rental markets in the country where zoning permits STR use. Our DSCR program supports STR income scenarios.

signals.title: Constrained supply
signals.body: Hawaii's islands have inherently constrained developable land supply, which supports long-term property values and rental rates over the hold period.

signals.title: Deep tourism + military demand
signals.body: Tourism-driven employment and consistent military stationing (Pearl Harbor, Schofield, MCB Hawaii) anchor sustained rental demand across Oahu in particular.

states.whyInvestor: Hawaii is a specialty market. STR + LTR mixed strategies work in resort corridors where zoning permits. Constrained land supply supports long-term property values. Military and tourism employment anchors rental demand on Oahu especially.

states.name: Idaho

metros.name: Boise Metro
metros.detail: Meridian, Nampa, Caldwell, Eagle — fastest-growing corridor

metros.name: Coeur d'Alene
metros.detail: Kootenai County — Northern Idaho + Spokane adjacencies

metros.name: Twin Falls
metros.detail: Twin Falls County — Magic Valley SFR

metros.name: Lewiston
metros.detail: Nez Perce County — Snake River corridor

signals.title: Sustained migration inflow
signals.body: Idaho has been among the fastest-growing states in the US by net domestic migration for years. Rental demand across Boise metro in particular stays deep.

signals.title: Boise metro growth
signals.body: Meridian and Nampa are among the fastest-growing cities in the country. Employment and population growth support long-term rental demand across the Treasure Valley.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly
signals.body: Idaho statutes are landlord-friendly with predictable eviction timelines. Holding costs stay projectable across the state.

states.whyInvestor: Idaho is a top-inflow state. Boise metro (Meridian, Nampa, Eagle) has been among the fastest-growing corridors in the country. Sustained migration and job growth anchor rental demand. Landlord-friendly law rounds it out.

states.name: Illinois

metros.name: Chicago Metro
metros.detail: Cook County + suburban Chicago (Lake, DuPage, Will counties)

metros.name: Peoria
metros.detail: Peoria County — Central Illinois + Caterpillar demand

metros.name: Quad Cities (IL side)
metros.detail: Rock Island, Moline — cross-river metro with Iowa

signals.title: Chicago metro depth
signals.body: Cook County and suburban Chicago (Lake, DuPage, Will, Kane counties) offer deep SFR + small-multi inventory with real cash-flow at the right basis.

signals.title: University + employer anchors
signals.body: Champaign-Urbana (Illinois), Bloomington-Normal (State Farm + ISU), and Peoria (Caterpillar) anchor stable rental demand outside Chicago.

signals.title: Cash-flow secondary markets
signals.body: Rockford, Peoria, and Springfield offer some of the highest cash-on-cash yields in the Midwest — attractive for portfolio investors focused on income vs. appreciation.

states.whyInvestor: Illinois is a two-market state for investors: Chicago metro depth for scale and long-term hold value, plus Rockford / Peoria / Springfield secondary markets for pure cash-flow yield. Deep SFR inventory across the state supports every strategy.

states.name: Indiana

metros.name: Indianapolis Metro
metros.detail: Marion County + Carmel, Fishers, Noblesville, Greenwood, Avon

metros.name: South Bend
metros.detail: St. Joseph County — Notre Dame demand + Michiana

metros.name: Terre Haute & Muncie
metros.detail: Central Indiana secondary markets — Ball State + ISU demand

signals.title: Affordable acquisition basis
signals.body: Indianapolis and Indiana secondary markets offer entry price-points that make the yield math work — real cash-on-cash returns even after debt service.

signals.title: Central Indiana growth
signals.body: Metro Indianapolis has seen sustained employment and population growth, with logistics, life sciences, and manufacturing employers anchoring long-term rental demand.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly
signals.body: Indiana statutes are considered landlord-friendly with predictable eviction timelines. Holding costs stay projectable for cash-flow modeling.

states.whyInvestor: Indiana is one of the deeper cash-flow rental markets in the Midwest. Affordable acquisition basis across Indianapolis, Fort Wayne, and secondary markets drives real cash-on-cash yields. Central Indiana continues to see population and employment growth. Landlord-friendly statutes support projectable holds.

states.name: Iowa

metros.name: Des Moines Metro
metros.detail: Polk + Dallas counties — insurance + fintech hub

metros.name: Iowa City
metros.detail: Johnson County — University of Iowa + hospital demand

signals.title: Des Moines fintech + insurance
signals.body: Des Moines is one of the largest insurance hubs in the US and hosts a growing fintech sector (Principal, Wells Fargo campus, EMC Insurance). Stable employment drives rental demand.

signals.title: Affordable basis + high yield
signals.body: Iowa secondary markets offer some of the highest cash-on-cash yields in the Midwest — attractive for portfolio investors focused on income.

signals.title: University anchors
signals.body: Iowa City (U of Iowa + hospital system) and Ames (Iowa State) provide stable, university-anchored rental demand.

states.whyInvestor: Iowa is a strong cash-flow Midwest market. Des Moines anchors on insurance + fintech employment. University demand (Iowa City, Ames) adds stability. Secondary markets deliver high cash-on-cash yields for portfolio investors.

states.name: Kansas

metros.name: Overland Park & Olathe
metros.detail: Johnson County — Kansas City metro (KS side)

metros.name: Manhattan
metros.detail: Riley County — Kansas State + Fort Riley demand

metros.name: Kansas City (KS)
metros.detail: Wyandotte County — KC metro west side

signals.title: Johnson County commuter belt
signals.body: Overland Park, Olathe, Leawood, and Lenexa are among the fastest-growing high-income suburbs in the KC metro. Deep rental demand for professionals working in Kansas City.

signals.title: Wichita aviation anchor
signals.body: Wichita hosts Textron Aviation, Spirit AeroSystems, and a deep aerospace manufacturing base. Stable employment drives long-term rental demand.

signals.title: University + military anchors
signals.body: Lawrence (KU) and Manhattan (Kansas State + Fort Riley) provide stable, university- and military-anchored rental demand.

states.whyInvestor: Kansas is a mixed investor market. Johnson County commuter suburbs deliver premium LTR to KC-metro professionals. Wichita aviation industry anchors long-term demand. Lawrence and Manhattan add university + military stability.

states.name: Kentucky

metros.name: Louisville Metro
metros.detail: Jefferson County + St. Matthews, Middletown, PRP, Jeffersontown

metros.name: Bowling Green
metros.detail: Warren County — WKU + Corvette / GM plant demand

metros.name: Northern Kentucky
metros.detail: Covington, Newport, Florence — Cincinnati metro adjacencies

metros.name: Owensboro
metros.detail: Daviess County — Ohio River SFR market

metros.name: Elizabethtown
metros.detail: Hardin County — Fort Knox demand + Louisville-adjacent

signals.title: Louisville logistics anchor
signals.body: Louisville hosts UPS Worldport (the world's largest air-freight hub) and multiple Ford truck plants. That anchors long-term employment and steady rental demand across the metro.

signals.title: Lexington & UK
signals.body: Lexington combines horse-country + University of Kentucky + a growing tech and healthcare base. Rental demand stays deep and predictable.

signals.title: Affordable, projectable holds
signals.body: Kentucky acquisition basis stays affordable relative to gross rents. Non-judicial foreclosure and standard eviction timelines keep holds projectable.

states.whyInvestor: Louisville logistics anchor (UPS Worldport + Ford) drives steady rental demand. Lexington adds horse-country + UK + healthcare. Acquisition basis stays affordable relative to gross rents, and non-judicial foreclosure keeps holds projectable.

states.name: Louisiana

metros.name: New Orleans Metro
metros.detail: Orleans + Jefferson parishes — LTR + STR corridors

metros.name: Baton Rouge
metros.detail: East Baton Rouge Parish — state capital + LSU

signals.title: New Orleans tourism + STR
signals.body: New Orleans hosts one of the most active STR markets in the South where zoning permits. Our DSCR program supports STR income on qualifying properties.

signals.title: Baton Rouge + LSU
signals.body: Baton Rouge combines state-capital employment with LSU + the LSU medical complex, driving stable long-term rental demand.

signals.title: Petrochemical + industrial anchors
signals.body: Lake Charles, Baton Rouge, and the Mississippi River industrial corridor host deep petrochemical and industrial employment that supports steady rental demand.

states.whyInvestor: Louisiana is a mixed-strategy market. New Orleans supports LTR + STR mixed portfolios. Baton Rouge combines state-capital + LSU stability. Industrial corridors along the Mississippi anchor cash-flow SFR.

states.name: Maine

metros.name: Portland Metro
metros.detail: Cumberland County — Portland + South Portland + Westbrook

metros.name: Brunswick
metros.detail: Cumberland County — Bowdoin + former NAS Brunswick redevelopment

signals.title: Portland growth
signals.body: Portland has become one of the fastest-growing metros in the Northeast, with tech, healthcare, and food-and-beverage employment supporting deep rental demand across the metro.

signals.title: Coastal STR corridor
signals.body: Southern Maine and Midcoast Maine host active seasonal STR markets. Our DSCR program supports STR income on qualifying properties.

signals.title: Constrained supply
signals.body: Maine housing supply is constrained by both geography and slow permitting timelines, which supports long-term property values and rental rates.

states.whyInvestor: Portland has grown into one of the most in-demand Northeast metros. Coastal STR corridors add seasonal income flexibility. Constrained supply supports long-term value.

states.name: Maryland

metros.name: Prince George's County
metros.detail: DC-metro commuter belt + Bowie, Laurel, Upper Marlboro

metros.name: Montgomery County
metros.detail: Rockville, Silver Spring, Gaithersburg, Bethesda — DC-metro

metros.name: Annapolis
metros.detail: Anne Arundel County — state capital + USNA demand

signals.title: DC-metro commuter demand
signals.body: Prince George's and Montgomery counties are core DC-metro commuter belts. Rental demand from federal workers, contractors, and biotech/pharma employees stays consistent.

signals.title: Federal + biotech anchors
signals.body: The Maryland side of the DC metro hosts NIH, FDA, NSA (Fort Meade), and a deep biotech corridor (Bethesda, Rockville, Gaithersburg). Stable long-term employment.

signals.title: Baltimore SFR depth
signals.body: Baltimore City and County offer deep SFR inventory with real cash-flow opportunity for portfolio investors focused on income.

states.whyInvestor: Maryland delivers two rental strategies: DC-metro commuter belts (Prince George's, Montgomery) for premium LTR to federal/biotech workers, and Baltimore SFR for cash-flow yield. Both work — pick your play.

states.name: Massachusetts

metros.name: Springfield
metros.detail: Hampden County — Western MA + cash-flow markets

signals.title: Boston tech + biotech
signals.body: Kendall Square, the Longwood Medical Area, and Route 128 anchor the deepest biotech and tech employer bases in the country outside Silicon Valley. Rental demand stays premium.

signals.title: University market density
signals.body: Boston metro hosts more universities per capita than any US metro. Cambridge, Boston University, Northeastern, and dozens more anchor consistent student and post-grad rental demand.

signals.title: Cash-flow secondaries
signals.body: Springfield, Worcester, New Bedford, and Fall River offer real cash-on-cash yields — the yield markets New England investors use to balance premium Boston-metro appreciation plays.

states.whyInvestor: Massachusetts pairs premium Boston-metro appreciation plays with cash-flow secondaries (Springfield, Worcester, Fall River, New Bedford). Biotech + tech employer depth is unmatched. Universities anchor rental demand across every metro.

states.name: Michigan

metros.name: Detroit Metro
metros.detail: Wayne + Oakland + Macomb counties — SFR + urban

metros.name: Grand Rapids
metros.detail: Kent County — West Michigan growth market

metros.name: Ann Arbor
metros.detail: Washtenaw County — University of Michigan + tech

signals.title: Detroit auto + EV comeback
signals.body: The Detroit metro has been rebuilding around EV manufacturing (Ford, GM, Stellantis), tech, and financial services (Rocket, Dan Gilbert investments). Rental demand across the metro has strengthened.

signals.title: Grand Rapids growth
signals.body: Grand Rapids has been one of the fastest-growing Midwest metros over the past decade, with medical, education, and manufacturing employers anchoring long-term demand.

signals.title: University + cash-flow mix
signals.body: Ann Arbor (Michigan) and East Lansing (MSU) provide premium university-anchored demand. Detroit and Flint offer some of the highest cash-flow yields in the Midwest.

states.whyInvestor: Michigan pairs Detroit metro EV/auto comeback with Grand Rapids growth and Ann Arbor / East Lansing university stability. Detroit and Flint offer some of the highest cash-flow SFR yields in the country for portfolio investors.

states.name: Mississippi

metros.name: Jackson Metro
metros.detail: Hinds + Madison + Rankin counties — state capital

metros.name: Gulfport & Biloxi
metros.detail: Harrison County — Gulf Coast SFR + STR

metros.name: Hattiesburg
metros.detail: Forrest County — Southern Mississippi + USM demand

metros.name: Starkville & Oxford
metros.detail: Mississippi State + Ole Miss university markets

signals.title: Affordable acquisition basis
signals.body: Mississippi acquisition basis stays among the lowest in the country relative to gross rents. Real cash-on-cash returns for portfolio investors focused on income.

signals.title: Memphis-metro spillover
signals.body: DeSoto County (Southaven, Olive Branch, Hernando) is effectively part of the Memphis metro. Rental demand from Memphis-area professionals crossing the state line stays consistent.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly
signals.body: Mississippi statutes are landlord-friendly with predictable timelines. Holding costs stay projectable across the state.

states.whyInvestor: Mississippi is one of the highest-yield SFR markets in the country. DeSoto County spillover from Memphis, Jackson state-capital demand, and Gulf Coast SFR round out a strong cash-flow strategy set.

states.name: Missouri

metros.name: Kansas City Metro
metros.detail: Jackson County + Lee's Summit, Independence, Blue Springs, Overland Park (KS)

metros.name: St. Louis
metros.detail: St. Louis City + County — Clayton, University City, Kirkwood

metros.name: Columbia
metros.detail: Boone County — University of Missouri market

signals.title: Kansas City metro growth
signals.body: KC has seen sustained metro growth and remains one of the more affordable major metros in the country by rent-to-price ratio. Strong logistics + biotech + tech employers.

signals.title: St. Louis SFR depth
signals.body: St. Louis offers deep SFR inventory at low entry basis — one of the highest cash-on-cash yield markets in the country for buy-and-hold investors.

signals.title: Predictable holding costs
signals.body: Missouri's eviction and foreclosure processes are predictable. Landlord-friendly statutes keep holds projectable for portfolio investors.

states.whyInvestor: Missouri is a strong cash-flow Midwest market. Kansas City remains one of the more affordable major metros by rent-to-price ratio. St. Louis offers deep SFR inventory with real cash-on-cash yields. Predictable eviction + foreclosure timelines round it out.

states.name: Montana

metros.name: Missoula
metros.detail: Missoula County — University of Montana + growth

metros.name: Helena
metros.detail: Lewis and Clark County — state capital

metros.name: Butte & Anaconda
metros.detail: Historic mining region — cash-flow secondary markets

signals.title: Bozeman + Missoula growth
signals.body: Bozeman has been one of the fastest-growing US micropolitans for years. Missoula adds sustained U of Montana + healthcare demand. Rental demand along Montana's I-90 corridor stays deep.

signals.title: Migration inflow
signals.body: Montana continues to attract in-migration from higher-cost coastal metros — supporting steady rental demand across the state's main metros.

signals.title: Glacier + Yellowstone STR
signals.body: Kalispell, Whitefish, and Bozeman host active STR markets adjacent to Glacier and Yellowstone. Our DSCR program supports STR income where zoning permits.

states.whyInvestor: Montana pairs sustained growth (Bozeman, Missoula) with STR opportunity in Glacier and Yellowstone-adjacent corridors. Migration inflow keeps rental demand strong across the I-90 corridor.

states.name: Nebraska

metros.name: Omaha Metro
metros.detail: Douglas + Sarpy counties — insurance + fintech hub

metros.name: Lincoln
metros.detail: Lancaster County — state capital + Nebraska Huskers

signals.title: Omaha insurance + fintech
signals.body: Omaha is home to Berkshire Hathaway, Mutual of Omaha, Union Pacific, and a growing fintech and data-center cluster. Stable long-term employment drives rental demand.

signals.title: University + military anchors
signals.body: Lincoln (UNL) and Bellevue (Offutt AFB) provide stable university- and military-anchored demand across the metro belt.

signals.title: Affordable + high-yield
signals.body: Nebraska acquisition basis stays affordable relative to gross rents. Cash-on-cash yields make sense for portfolio investors focused on income.

states.whyInvestor: Nebraska is anchored by Omaha's Fortune-500 employer base (Berkshire Hathaway, Mutual of Omaha, Union Pacific) plus fintech growth. Lincoln adds state-capital + UNL stability. Yields make sense for income-focused portfolios.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.taxNote: no state income tax on wages

signals.title: No state income tax on wages
signals.body: New Hampshire has no income tax on wages — a real benefit for rental owners running properties here. (Investment income tax on dividends and interest exists but has been phasing out.)

signals.title: Boston-metro commuter belt
signals.body: Nashua, Manchester, and Salem are core Boston-metro commuter markets. Rental demand from professionals working in Massachusetts stays consistent.

signals.title: Seacoast tech + defense
signals.body: Portsmouth and the Seacoast host tech (Bottomline Technologies) and defense (Portsmouth Naval Shipyard) employment that supports long-term rental demand.

states.whyInvestor: New Hampshire pairs no-income-tax-on-wages advantage with Boston-metro commuter demand (Nashua, Salem, Manchester). Seacoast tech + defense employment anchors long-term rentals. Constrained supply supports property values.

states.name: New Jersey

metros.name: Newark & Jersey City
metros.detail: Essex + Hudson counties — NYC-metro premium LTR

metros.name: Bergen County
metros.detail: Hackensack, Fort Lee, Paramus — NYC commuter belt

metros.name: Middlesex County
metros.detail: New Brunswick, Edison, Woodbridge — pharma + Rutgers

metros.name: Monmouth County
metros.detail: Red Bank, Freehold, Long Branch — shore corridor

signals.title: NYC + Philly metro depth
signals.body: New Jersey is uniquely positioned between two of the largest US metros. Bergen, Hudson, Essex counties feed NYC; Camden and Cherry Hill feed Philadelphia. Deep dual-market rental demand.

signals.title: Pharma + finance anchors
signals.body: Central New Jersey hosts one of the deepest pharma employer bases in the world (Merck, J&J, Bristol Myers). Northern New Jersey adds finance and tech. Stable long-term employment.

signals.title: Shore STR corridor
signals.body: The Jersey Shore hosts one of the most active summer STR markets in the country. Our DSCR program supports STR income where zoning permits.

states.whyInvestor: New Jersey feeds two Fortune-500 metros (NYC + Philadelphia) with commuter-belt rentals. Central Jersey pharma anchors long-term stability. Shore corridors add STR income flexibility.

states.name: New Mexico

metros.name: Santa Fe
metros.detail: Santa Fe County — state capital + arts market

metros.name: Las Cruces
metros.detail: Doña Ana County — Southern NM + NMSU

metros.name: Farmington
metros.detail: San Juan County — Four Corners energy market

signals.title: Sandia + Los Alamos anchors
signals.body: Albuquerque and Santa Fe rental demand is anchored by Sandia National Labs and Los Alamos National Laboratory — stable federal employment for the long term.

signals.title: Kirtland AFB
signals.body: Kirtland Air Force Base in Albuquerque supports steady military rental demand.

signals.title: STR corridors
signals.body: Santa Fe and Taos host active STR markets. Our DSCR program supports STR income where zoning permits.

states.whyInvestor: New Mexico anchors on federal employment (Sandia, Los Alamos, Kirtland AFB). Santa Fe and Taos add STR opportunity. Affordable acquisition basis supports real cash-flow across ABQ and secondary markets.

states.name: New York

metros.name: NYC Outer Boroughs
metros.detail: Bronx, Queens, Brooklyn, Staten Island — SFR + small-multi

metros.name: Westchester County
metros.detail: White Plains, Yonkers, New Rochelle — NYC-metro

metros.name: Buffalo
metros.detail: Erie County — Western NY high-yield SFR

metros.name: Syracuse
metros.detail: Onondaga County — Central NY + SU market

signals.title: NYC-metro commuter depth
signals.body: Long Island, Westchester, and the outer boroughs feed NYC's employer base — one of the deepest premium-rental markets in the country. Demand stays constant across every cycle.

signals.title: Upstate cash-flow markets
signals.body: Rochester, Buffalo, and Syracuse offer some of the highest cash-on-cash yields in the Northeast. Real income for portfolio investors focused on cash flow vs. appreciation.

signals.title: University + healthcare anchors
signals.body: Rochester (U of Rochester + Rochester Institute of Technology), Syracuse (SU), and Albany (SUNY + state government) provide stable rental demand outside NYC-metro.

states.whyInvestor: New York State runs two rental strategies: NYC-metro commuter belts (Long Island, Westchester) for premium LTR to Manhattan workers, and Upstate NY (Rochester, Buffalo, Syracuse) for high cash-flow yield. Both work at scale.

states.name: North Carolina

metros.name: Charlotte Metro
metros.detail: Concord, Huntersville, Matthews, Mint Hill, Fort Mill (SC-adjacent)

metros.name: Raleigh
metros.detail: Cary, Apex, Wake Forest, Garner, Holly Springs

metros.name: Greensboro
metros.detail: Piedmont Triad — plus High Point, Kernersville

metros.name: Asheville
metros.detail: Buncombe County — LTR + mountain STR corridor

metros.name: Wilmington
metros.detail: Coastal SFR, plus Leland, Hampstead, Wrightsville

signals.title: Charlotte + Triangle job engines
signals.body: Charlotte is a top-five US banking hub. The Research Triangle (Raleigh-Durham-Chapel Hill) anchors an unusually dense concentration of tech, biotech, and higher-education employers. Both metros pull in steady, high-income renters.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly, non-judicial
signals.body: North Carolina is a non-judicial foreclosure state with a predictable eviction process and landlord-friendly statutes. Holding costs stay projectable for cash-flow modeling.

signals.title: Sustained population inflow
signals.body: NC has ranked among the top states for net domestic migration for years running. That inflow shows up as sustained rental demand in every metro from the mountains to the coast.

states.whyInvestor: NC is a top-tier investor state. Charlotte and the Research Triangle are sustained-growth job markets attracting steady in-migration. Non-judicial foreclosure and landlord-friendly statutes keep timelines predictable. Rental demand is deep across every metro.

states.name: Ohio

metros.name: Columbus
metros.detail: Franklin County — New Albany, Pataskala, Dublin, Westerville (Intel corridor)

metros.name: Cleveland
metros.detail: Cuyahoga County — Lakewood, Parma, Cleveland Heights, Shaker Heights

metros.name: Cincinnati
metros.detail: Hamilton County — plus Northern Kentucky adjacencies

metros.name: Youngstown & Canton
metros.detail: Mahoning + Stark counties — yield-heavy secondary markets

signals.title: Affordable acquisition basis
signals.body: Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati, and Dayton all offer entry price-points well below coastal metros. That gap is where real cash-on-cash yields live for buy-and-hold investors.

signals.title: Intel + Columbus growth
signals.body: Intel's multi-billion-dollar semiconductor investment in Licking County is anchoring long-term employment growth around Columbus. Rental demand in the metro and its northeast suburbs has strengthened accordingly.

signals.title: Predictable holding costs
signals.body: Ohio's eviction process is straightforward and timelines run predictably. That matters when you're modeling cash flow on a rental hold across multiple properties.

states.whyInvestor: Ohio is one of the strongest cash-flow rental markets in the country. Affordable acquisition basis relative to gross rents drives real cash-on-cash yields across Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati, and Dayton. Predictable eviction timelines keep holds projectable.

states.name: Oklahoma

metros.name: Oklahoma City Metro
metros.detail: Oklahoma County + Edmond, Norman, Moore, Yukon

metros.name: Tulsa Metro
metros.detail: Tulsa County + Broken Arrow, Owasso, Bixby

signals.title: Energy + aerospace anchors
signals.body: Oklahoma City and Tulsa host deep energy (Devon, Chesapeake) and aerospace (Boeing, American Airlines Tulsa MRO) employer bases. Stable long-term employment supports rental demand.

signals.title: Affordable + high-yield
signals.body: Oklahoma acquisition basis stays among the lowest relative to gross rents in the country. Real cash-on-cash returns for buy-and-hold investors.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly
signals.body: Oklahoma statutes are landlord-friendly with predictable eviction timelines. Holding costs stay projectable.

states.whyInvestor: Oklahoma is one of the highest-yield SFR markets in the country. OKC + Tulsa energy and aerospace anchors drive stable demand. Fort Sill (Lawton) and OU/OSU provide military + university stability. Landlord-friendly law rounds it out.

states.name: Oregon

metros.name: Eugene
metros.detail: Lane County — University of Oregon market

metros.name: Bend
metros.detail: Deschutes County — Central Oregon growth + STR corridor

signals.title: Silicon Forest tech anchor
signals.body: Washington County (Beaverton, Hillsboro) hosts Intel's largest US site plus Nike's global HQ. Deep tech + design employment drives rental demand across the metro.

signals.title: Bend growth + STR
signals.body: Bend has been one of the fastest-growing metros in the Pacific Northwest. Active STR corridors on the east side of the Cascades add flexibility. Our DSCR program supports STR income where zoning permits.

signals.title: University + capital demand
signals.body: Eugene (Oregon), Corvallis (OSU), and Salem (state capital) round out stable, predictable rental demand outside the Portland metro.

states.whyInvestor: Oregon is anchored by Portland's Silicon Forest (Intel, Nike) plus growing Central Oregon (Bend). Universities and state-capital demand round out the state. STR corridors add flexibility for mixed strategies.

states.name: Pennsylvania

metros.name: Erie
metros.detail: Erie County — Northwest PA cash-flow market

metros.name: Scranton & Wilkes-Barre
metros.detail: Northeast PA — NYC/Philly overflow + cash-flow SFR

signals.title: Pittsburgh tech + healthcare
signals.body: Pittsburgh has repositioned around Carnegie Mellon-driven robotics + AI, plus a deep healthcare employer base (UPMC). Steady, high-quality employment drives rental demand.

signals.title: Lehigh Valley logistics
signals.body: The Lehigh Valley (Allentown, Bethlehem, Easton) has become one of the largest East Coast logistics and distribution hubs — Amazon, FedEx, and dozens of anchor tenants. Long-term rental demand.

signals.title: Deep cash-flow secondaries
signals.body: Erie, Scranton, Wilkes-Barre, and Harrisburg offer some of the highest cash-on-cash yields in the Northeast. Real income for portfolio investors focused on cash flow.

states.whyInvestor: Pennsylvania pairs Philadelphia + Pittsburgh anchor metros with a deep set of cash-flow secondaries (Erie, Scranton, Wilkes-Barre, Harrisburg). Lehigh Valley logistics adds a major growth angle. Real yield markets for portfolio investors.

states.name: Rhode Island

metros.name: Newport
metros.detail: Newport County — LTR + coastal STR corridor

metros.name: Kingston & Narragansett
metros.detail: Washington County — URI + South County beach

signals.title: Providence university density
signals.body: Providence hosts Brown University, RISD, Providence College, and Johnson & Wales. Deep student and post-grad rental demand year-round.

signals.title: Newport STR corridor
signals.body: Newport is one of the most active summer STR markets in the Northeast where zoning permits. Our DSCR program supports STR income scenarios.

signals.title: Boston-metro spillover
signals.body: Providence has emerged as a real Boston-metro spillover market as Massachusetts rents rise. Rental demand from Massachusetts commuters stays consistent.

states.whyInvestor: Rhode Island packs a lot into a small footprint: Providence university density drives constant rental demand, Newport supports high-value STR, and the state has become a Boston-metro spillover for cost-conscious commuters.

states.name: South Carolina

metros.name: Charleston
metros.detail: Charleston County — plus Mount Pleasant, North Charleston, Summerville

metros.name: Columbia
metros.detail: Richland + Lexington counties — capital + Fort Jackson demand

metros.name: Myrtle Beach
metros.detail: Horry County — coastal LTR + STR corridor

metros.name: Hilton Head & Bluffton
metros.detail: Beaufort County — premium coastal STR + LTR

signals.title: Sustained migration inflow
signals.body: South Carolina has ranked among the top US states for net migration for years. That inflow shows up as sustained rental demand across every major metro.

signals.title: Greenville manufacturing anchor
signals.body: BMW's largest global plant, Michelin's North American HQ, and a deep automotive supply chain anchor Upstate SC's rental demand around Greenville and Spartanburg.

signals.title: Coastal STR opportunity
signals.body: Myrtle Beach, Hilton Head, Folly Beach, and Charleston's beach corridors host active short-term rental markets — SLA's DSCR program supports STR income where local zoning permits.

states.whyInvestor: SC is one of the top-inflow states in the country. Greenville is a major manufacturing hub (BMW/Michelin) driving stable employment. Charleston combines port-city commercial activity with a strong coastal STR market. Landlord-friendly law rounds it out.

states.name: Tennessee

metros.name: Nashville Metro
metros.detail: Franklin, Brentwood, Murfreesboro, Hendersonville, Mount Juliet, Spring Hill

metros.name: Memphis
metros.detail: Shelby County — high-yield SFR corridor; DeSoto MS adjacent

metros.name: Knoxville
metros.detail: Knox County — Farragut, Maryville, Oak Ridge

metros.name: Chattanooga
metros.detail: Hamilton County — plus Signal Mountain, East Ridge

signals.title: No state income tax
signals.body: Tennessee has no state income tax on rental income or capital gains — a meaningful yield boost that compounds over the hold period.

signals.title: Nashville growth + Memphis yield
signals.body: Nashville has been on a decade-long run of population, employment, and rent growth. Memphis offers some of the highest cash-on-cash SFR yields in the country. Both work — pick the strategy.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly
signals.body: Tennessee statutes are considered landlord-friendly with predictable eviction and non-judicial foreclosure processes. Holding costs stay projectable for cash-flow modeling.

states.whyInvestor: TN is a top-tier investor state. No state income tax. Nashville has been on a decade-long growth run; Memphis offers some of the highest cash-flow SFR yields in the country. Landlord-friendly statutes support projectable holds.

states.name: Texas

metros.name: Houston
metros.detail: Greater Houston metro — including Sugar Land, The Woodlands, Katy, Pearland

metros.name: Dallas–Fort Worth
metros.detail: DFW metroplex — Arlington, Plano, Frisco, McKinney, Denton, Irving

metros.name: San Antonio
metros.detail: San Antonio–New Braunfels — plus Schertz, Cibolo, Universal City

metros.name: Austin
metros.detail: Austin–Round Rock — including Cedar Park, Georgetown, Pflugerville

signals.title: No state income tax
signals.body: Rental income and capital gains from Texas properties are not subject to state-level income tax. That's a real yield boost that compounds over the life of the hold.

signals.title: Landlord-friendly statutes
signals.body: Texas allows non-judicial eviction under standard lease breaches and typical timelines run 30–45 days from filing to writ of possession. No rent control at the state level.

signals.title: Migration & job growth
signals.body: Texas continues to lead the country in domestic migration inflow. Dallas-Fort Worth, Houston, Austin, and San Antonio have all seen sustained population and employment growth that supports rental demand.

states.whyInvestor: Texas is one of the largest and most active rental investment markets in the United States. The state has no state income tax, landlord-friendly statutes (including a streamlined non-judicial eviction process), and continued population and job growth across major metros — all of which support strong rental demand.

states.name: Virginia

metros.name: Northern Virginia
metros.detail: Fairfax + Loudoun + Prince William — DC-metro + data-center corridor

metros.name: Hampton Roads
metros.detail: Virginia Beach, Norfolk, Chesapeake, Newport News — military-anchored

signals.title: NoVA federal + data centers
signals.body: Northern Virginia hosts the deepest federal + defense-contractor employer base in the country outside DC itself, plus the world's largest data-center corridor (Loudoun County). Stable, high-income rental demand.

signals.title: Hampton Roads military anchor
signals.body: Norfolk Naval Base, Naval Station Norfolk, Langley AFB, and multiple military installations anchor deep military rental demand across Hampton Roads.

signals.title: University anchors
signals.body: Charlottesville (UVA), Blacksburg (Virginia Tech), and Williamsburg (William & Mary) provide stable university-anchored rental demand across the state.

states.whyInvestor: Virginia combines Northern Virginia federal + data-center employment with Hampton Roads military demand and Richmond state-capital stability. Universities round out predictable long-term rental demand across the state.

states.name: Washington
states.taxNote: no state income tax on wages

metros.name: Seattle Metro
metros.detail: King County — plus Bellevue, Redmond, Kirkland (tech corridor)

metros.name: Tacoma
metros.detail: Pierce County — Seattle-adjacent SFR + JBLM demand

metros.name: Spokane
metros.detail: Spokane County — Eastern Washington + Idaho adjacencies

metros.name: Bellingham
metros.detail: Whatcom County — WWU + Canadian border market

signals.title: No state income tax on wages
signals.body: Washington has no state income tax on wages — a meaningful yield boost for investment property owners over the hold period.

signals.title: Seattle-metro tech anchors
signals.body: Amazon, Microsoft, Boeing, plus a deep enterprise software ecosystem (Bellevue, Redmond, Kirkland) anchor premium rental demand across the Seattle metro.

signals.title: JBLM + defense
signals.body: Joint Base Lewis-McChord (JBLM) is one of the largest military bases on the West Coast. Anchor military rental demand across Tacoma and Pierce County.

states.whyInvestor: Washington pairs no-state-income-tax advantage with Seattle-metro tech premium rentals (Amazon, Microsoft, Boeing). Tacoma + JBLM add military stability. Spokane and Vancouver round out the state with secondary market opportunities.

states.name: West Virginia

metros.name: Huntington
metros.detail: Cabell County — Ohio River + Marshall University

metros.name: Martinsburg
metros.detail: Berkeley County — DC-metro commuter belt (Eastern Panhandle)

signals.title: DC-metro Eastern Panhandle
signals.body: Martinsburg and Charles Town (Jefferson County) have become active DC-metro commuter markets. Rental demand from federal workers seeking lower cost of living stays consistent.

signals.title: Morgantown + WVU + Pittsburgh
signals.body: Morgantown combines WVU + healthcare + Pittsburgh-metro adjacency. Rental demand from students, healthcare workers, and Pittsburgh commuters supports the market.

signals.title: Affordable + high-yield
signals.body: West Virginia acquisition basis stays among the lowest in the country relative to gross rents. Real cash-on-cash returns for portfolio investors focused on income.

states.whyInvestor: West Virginia delivers surprising investor value. Eastern Panhandle (Martinsburg) is a DC-metro commuter belt. Morgantown pairs WVU with Pittsburgh-metro adjacency. Statewide, WV offers some of the highest cash-flow yields in the country.

states.name: Wisconsin

metros.name: Eau Claire
metros.detail: Eau Claire County — Western Wisconsin + UWEC

signals.title: Madison + UW anchor
signals.body: Madison combines state-capital employment with UW-Madison (one of the largest research universities in the US) and Epic Systems (healthcare software HQ). Deep, stable rental demand.

signals.title: Kenosha/Racine Chicago-metro
signals.body: Southeast Wisconsin's Kenosha and Racine counties feed Chicago-metro employers — rental demand from Illinois commuters seeking Wisconsin cost of living.

signals.title: Manufacturing anchors
signals.body: Green Bay, Appleton, and the Fox Valley host deep paper + manufacturing employer bases that support long-term rental demand.

states.whyInvestor: Wisconsin pairs Madison stability (state capital + UW + Epic) with Milwaukee metro depth and Southeast WI Chicago-metro spillover. Manufacturing anchors keep secondary-market yields real for portfolio investors.

states.name: Wyoming

metros.name: Cheyenne
metros.detail: Laramie County — state capital + F.E. Warren AFB

signals.title: No state income tax
signals.body: Wyoming has no state income tax on rental income — a real yield advantage. The state also has some of the most favorable business + trust laws in the country.

signals.title: Jackson STR corridor
signals.body: Jackson (Teton County) hosts one of the most active premium STR markets in the country adjacent to Grand Teton and Yellowstone. Our DSCR program supports STR income where zoning permits.

signals.title: Federal + energy anchors
signals.body: Cheyenne (F.E. Warren AFB, state capital) and the Powder River Basin (energy) provide stable employment across the state.

states.whyInvestor: Wyoming pairs no-state-income-tax advantage with premium Jackson STR opportunity and federal + energy employment anchors. Small but investor-friendly state with real business + trust law advantages.
